text: That's a smashing good joke, I think.

Tyranno-
saurus wrecks!
Is it true that a monster can't hurt you if you carry a torch?
Actually, it depends on how fast you carry it!
Do you know what time it is when Otto walks through your door?
So you've met Otto already.
Time to get a new door!
Do you know the difference between a Cheetaur and a comma?
Spoilsport!
A Cheetaur has claws at the end of its paws, and a comma is a pause at the end of a clause!

Do you know what's big, purple, and eats ungefaehr-
liche dinge?
Well...let me figure out something tougher.

Big, purple ungefaehr-
liche dinge eaters!

Large purple ungefaehr-
liche dinge eaters are all too common in the forest these days.
Do you know which has more legs, one cow or no cow?
Too easy, huh?  Well, I'll just have to think of a better one.
You're right!  NO cow has more than four legs.  These are too easy.
Do you know the difference between a wounded Triceratops and a magic user casting the "Fly" spell?
Darn.  I thought I made that one up myself!
One is a raging Saurus and the other is a soaring Magus!
Sorry, Fenrus, but "He who tells the jokes gets to make up the punch lines".
Did you hear the joke about the witches broom?
Oh.  That's a pity!
That's odd.  It's sweeping the valley!
"I'M BAAD!"
Never met her.  I only built this vacation home here a century ago.
Erana has a nice little magical meadow in her name northeast of here.  Pretty respectable Spellcaster, I imagine.
The Protection spell she cast over the town is fairly good, but I believe she missed a few places.
I thought that "Erasmus' Rootin' Tootin' Root Beer" was a WONDERFUL name for a product!  It's a shame the company went broke after that.
Erana's spell prevents the use of excessive violence or hostile magic within Spielburg's walls, with the exception of a couple of places.
It looks to me from up here as if she missed the back part of the alley, and the spell applies only to areas in town above the ground.
Nobody asked you.
Zara has a real flair for the theatric.  Her method of greeting customers at her shop is showy but effective.
It's a pity she has no sense of humor.

Baba Yaga is good at curses and shape-
changing spells.  She has a nasty temper, so it's best to stay on her good side.
You have to watch her.  She cheats at cards.
She started it!  I just wanted to give her a taste of her own medicine.
The curse Baba Yaga placed upon the Baron von Spielburg was a fine example of creative cursing.
"Upon von Spielburg and all his clan, this Curse I now demand:   What I will shall come full measure, so shall ye lose all that ye treasure."
Of course, the problem is that for every curse there is an equal and opposite countercurse.
A countercurse is the cure for a curse.  It usually works against the one who cast the curse in the first place.
The countercurse for the curse on the Baron goes:
"Come a hero from the East..."
"Free the man from in the beast..."
"Bring the child from out the band..."
"Drive the Curser from the land."
So, to break the curse, a hero must get rid of Baba Yaga, in addition to all the other things.  It does not reflect well on Baba Yaga.
Henry is a hermit who lives by the Flying Falls to the south. Great sense of humor.
I think he borrowed something the last time I saw him, but I can't remember exactly what it was.
Oh, well, Henry is a great one to talk to.
You need to complete your quest here before you can become initiated as a Wizard.
There are many types of magic mirrors.  One of the more common types is the one that has a strange face popping up every so often.
That's not a magic mirror.
At any rate, I used to have a nice magic hand mirror, but I misplaced it somewhere.  It could be used to reflect a spell back upon the caster.
So, if you happen upon a small golden magic mirror with a dragon on the handle, you'll know it's mine.
Oh.  That's right!
Well, if you find a magic mirror with a face on it, you'll know it reflects spells.
Basically, anyone who uses magic is a magic user;   anyone who casts spells is a spellcaster.
However, in order to be a wizard, you need to have undergone initiation into the "Wizard's Institute of Technocery" in the South.
Magicians and sorcerers are wizards who specialize.

For myself, I prefer to master all aspects of Magic unlike the narrow-
minded specialists.
I prefer to think of myself as a dilettante.
He laughs too much when he casts his spells.
No, no!  That's a "neck romancer"!
A necromancer is a magic user who has a rather unsavory relationship with corpses.
It means, my dear Fenrus, that a necromancer is someone who deals with the dead.
Spades with shades?
"If Magic be the lute of life, play on!"
How about:  "Magic makes the world go 'round"?
Very well, then.  To put it in my own words:  "Magic is the essence and the soul of life, and the Wizard is Her poet".
Fenrus, there are times when you'd look better as a newt!
Because newts can't talk!
Just a little something to challenge the mind and the magic.  I like to play it every now and then, but Fenrus isn't much of a challenge anymore.
I still think you're cheating, somehow!
Anyway, the game involves casting four spells:   "Flame Dart", "Open", "Fetch", and "Trigger".
And to make it worth your while, if you beat me, I'll teach you a spell called "Dazzle".
The "Open" spell lets you open things with a mere gesture.
If you're really good at it, you can open locks.  It's useful when you lose your keys.
Why would they be there?
"Fetch" is a useful spell for catching and retrieving small objects at a distance.
It's good for putting things in their place.
I'd put you in your place, but I'm above such things.
The "Trigger" spell is used to set off an existing spell, just like pulling the spell's "trigger".
For example, if a chest has a magical trap on it, you can set off the trap by casting a "Trigger" spell.
Just make sure you're out of range of the trap.
I can set up other spells and let someone else "Trigger" them.
I fixed Henry the Hermit's cave for him so that he can do alot of magic with only one spell.
It's true name is Erasmus' Razzle Dazzle, by the way.
You cast the spell and anyone looking directly at its corruscating light is blinded for awhile and can't do anything.
Magical traps are a good way to keep thieves out of your chests and other places.
Put a fire trap, for example, on a chest.  When someone tries to open it, the thief will go up in smoke!
I only got singed the last time.  Nothing major.
A simple yes or no will suffice.
Come in and sit down.
Fenrus, our guest has arrived.
Since you are a practitioner of the magical arts, you might be interested in a little game I have.
I'll teach you the Dazzle spell if you win.  You do want to play, don't you?
Nice to see you back.  Shall we play a game of Mage's Maze?
Wonderful!  Then you can play a game of Mage's Maze with me.
Oh, well.  It is a good game.  Good practice, too.  Maybe we can play the next time you visit.
I believe I gave a learning scroll to the Hermit.  You should talk to him about it.
Too bad.  You really can't play the game without it.  You can buy a learning scroll at Zara's magic shop in town.
I so enjoy playing the Mage's Maze.
YUK-YUK-YUK-GUFFAW!
A-HYUK, A-HYUK, A-HYUK.  GAWRSH!
Tee-Hee-Hee!

HO-HO-HO-HEE-HEE-HEE
 HA-HA-HA!
The only thing I've seen the brigand warlock cast is sneezing powder.
It's important for a magic user to know as many spells as possible.  You never know what might come in handy.
They have a supposed warlock working with them.  From what I've seen, he's more nincompoop than necromancer.
I am a wizard of some reknown.
Please!  The least you could do is ask me about something more interesting.
I told you to be more interesting!  You may go now.

You seem to know all of my punchlines, so...
to SPELL with you!
Not in MY house you don't...
Well, if you really must go...
"Too much for you?  I guess you should work on your magic.  Maybe next time you'll be more of a challenge."
"Guess I still have a knack for the old Maze game."
"As I promised, I shall now teach you the spell known as `Erasmus' Razzle Dazzle'."
"Well!  You don't have to rub it in, you know!"
